feedback_captured_session_21: Plain English is mandatory; technical shorthand (CI, ephemeral, scaffold, pure router) must be translated before use
Orchestrator is NOT pure router — it writes state as progress meter per skill-creator + skill-audit precedent
Interactive menus throughout + utterance anywhere; top menu simple, depth in submenus

batch_2c_locked.q2c1_inventory_pass_scope: Walk every gitignored item reachable from project root (respecting .gitignore + .git/info/exclude + nested ignores). Cheap name-based fast-path classifies large directories (node_modules/, build outputs, etc.) as machine-local-by-design before content inspection — fast-path patterns live alongside Q18's pattern-based defaults in scripts/lib/.
batch_2c_locked.q2c2_classification_taxonomy: Three classes for v1: machine-local-by-design / should-sync / ambiguous. No fourth 'worth-surfacing-later' class — ambiguous absorbs that case (surface once, decide once, drop the ambiguity).
batch_2c_locked.q2c3_surfacing_rule: Triage file at .claude/state/context-sync-triage.jsonl, NOT mid-sync ask-on-new. /context-sync surfaces triage queue length on every invocation as a one-line 'N items pending triage' reminder. Q18's existing ask-on-new flow stays for files appearing AFTER first inventory (rare, worth interrupting).
batch_2c_locked.q2c4_should_sync_timing: v1. Inventory + classification + actual syncing of should-sync items all land together in v1 — no half-tool, per feedback_no_broken_widgets and feedback_no_mvp_defer_later.

Reasoning: every record self-describes; no external lookups. Second field-budget stretch after ledger 12→14, accepted because each is justified by Q17/Q25 'no null fields' discipline rather than scope creep.

delegation_decision_locked_session_22.decision: Option 1 — full delegation. Manual-port repo-analysis (and required shared/ + scripts/lib/ deps) from SoNash to JASON-OS as one-off bootstrap. Profile-discovery becomes a JASON-OS-specific TRANSFORMER (acknowledged 500-1000 lines, not 100-200) that invokes repo-analysis and re-shapes its output into JASON-OS profile records.
delegation_decision_locked_session_22.transformer_scope_acknowledged: User clarification: transformer is non-trivial. Three jobs: (1) re-frame Engineer View dimension findings as gate records with companion_directive plain-English text, (2) classify Deep Read enumerations into 5 unit types (file/family/memory/context-artifact/system) and infer 3 shape fields per type, (3) write the 9-field profile. The directive-text generation alone is the bulk of the work.
delegation_decision_locked_session_22.why_option_1_still_wins: The expensive layer is detection (9-phase pipeline, dimension agents, rate-limit safety, fork detection, monorepo handling). repo-analysis provides this intact. The transformer would exist in any option (even Option 3 'inspired-by' would need the same shape-inference + directive-generation code). Option 1 saves the bigger implementation cost.

rejected_options.option_2_targeted_reuse: Cherry-pick subsystems into scripts/lib/ — rejected: cherry-picking is meaningful work and forks repo-analysis's evolution from day one. User chose Option 1 explicitly.
rejected_options.option_3_inspired_by: Re-implement in JASON-OS using repo-analysis as inspiration only — rejected: matches the wheel-reinvention failure mode the user surfaced; explicitly NOT chosen.

weaknesses_accepted: Inheritance of SoNash opinions (TDMS, /synthesize integration, extraction-journal, etc.) — mitigated by inline keep/remove/edit walkthrough at port time.
Forks-over-time — mitigated long-term by /sync-back; short-term by manual periodic mirror.
Overhead for cheap cases — mitigated by repo-analysis's Quick depth tier.
delegation_decision_locked_session_22.next_step: Walkthrough COMPLETE 2026-04-25. All 8 batches locked, 61 decisions captured. Canonical record at .planning/cross-repo-movement-reframe/repo-analysis-port/PORT_DECISIONS.md. Next phase: actual port operation (file copy from SoNash + mechanical edit pass applying disposition table). After port lands, main-plan Batch 4 re-emerges as the smaller transformer-design batch.
